M-031.NAME: anti-ping-pong-abuse
M-031.owns: Redis-backed endpoint history, roaming-vs-ping-pong classification, and soft auto-rotation enforcement for shared AWG configs

exports.type-EndpointObservation: Normalized endpoint observation containing IP, observed_at, peer hash, user_id and device_id
exports.type-AntiAbuseDecision: Classifier result: ok, roaming, warning, ping_pong_abuse, multi_network_abuse, degraded
exports.fn-normalize-endpoint-ip: Extract IP from AWG endpoint and ignore NAT port-only changes
exports.fn-record-endpoint-observation: Append bounded TTL history for one peer in Redis
exports.fn-classify-endpoint-history: Detect A-B-A-B ping-pong and excessive unique IP churn while allowing sequential roaming
exports.fn-enforce-auto-rotate: Rotate one logical device config using existing device policy and VPN reprovisioning primitives

configuration.env-ANTI_ABUSE_MODE: disabled, observe, or auto_rotate; initial production rollout should start with observe

decisions.decision-1: Store short-lived endpoint history in Redis; persist only audit and enforcement events in Postgres.
decisions.decision-2: Ignore endpoint port changes and classify by normalized IP only.
decisions.decision-3: Auto-rotation is soft enforcement, not account ban; it invalidates the shared config and requires the user to download a new one.
decisions.decision-4: Redis outage degrades to observe-only logging and must not trigger auto-rotation.

implementation-status.done: Implemented anti_abuse classifier/store/enforcer, handshake monitor delegation, configurable scheduler interval, durable audit event types and admin anti-abuse context.
implementation-status.verification: V-M-031 passed on 2026-04-20: 24 passed, 12 warnings. Pytest was run outside sandbox because async SQLite/aiosqlite hangs inside sandbox.

M-031.hotspot: H-018: Auto-rotation must not punish normal Wi-Fi to LTE roaming, mobile carrier NAT churn, or Redis degradation.
